name: Philodendron 'Majestic'
origin: Horticultural cultivar; parentage from tropical Central and South American rainforest species (P. sodiroi × P. verrucosum)
aboutText: Philodendron 'Majestic' is a climbing horticultural hybrid believed to combine Philodendron sodiroi and Philodendron verrucosum — a pairing that produces one of the most visually distinctive Philodendrons in cultivation. From the sodiroi parent it inherits the remarkable satiny silver mottling that covers the deep green leaf surface, and from verrucosum it draws the velvety texture and bold, high-contrast pale venation that fans across the cordate blade. The result is a medium-to-large climber with broad, cordate leaves that are simultaneously velvety and silver-washed — an unusual double quality that sets 'Majestic' apart from either parent individually. Moderate internodes bear aerial roots at nodes for climbing and anchorage. New leaves emerge with the silver patterning most vivid, deepening slightly as the blade matures. A reliably popular and genuinely beautiful cultivar for anyone with the growing conditions to match its ambitions.

quickFacts.matureSize: Medium to Large climber (1-2.5m with support)

morphology.venation: Prominent pale midrib and radiating lateral veins on silver-mottled velvety deep green blade
morphology.variegation: None (silver mottling is species trait, not variegation)
morphology.growthHabit: Climbing with aerial roots at nodes, moderate internodes

propagation.notes: Single-node cuttings root readily in moist sphagnum or water. Maintain 70%+ humidity and bottom warmth for best results.

careGuide.substrate: High-porosity mix: 50% perlite, 30% orchid bark, 20% coco coir. Good aeration is essential — philodendrons are prone to root rot in dense substrates.
careGuide.watering: Water thoroughly when the top 3–4 cm of substrate is dry. Ensure free drainage and never allow to sit in water. Reduce frequency in winter.
careGuide.humidity: 60–80% preferred. UK tap water can cause brown tips — use rainwater or filtered water where possible.
careGuide.fertilising: Balanced liquid fertiliser (e.g. 20-20-20) at quarter strength every two weeks in spring and summer. Monthly at most in winter.
careGuide.repotting: Every 12–18 months or when roots circle the base of the pot. Move up one pot size only — oversized pots increase rot risk.

commonProblems.cause: Low humidity or fluoride/chlorine in tap water
commonProblems.fix: Increase ambient humidity and switch to rainwater or filtered water
Allow substrate to dry further between waterings; check root health and repot if necessary
Move to a brighter position with more indirect light

items: Check that roots are firm and white to tan — avoid plants with black, slimy, or absent roots
Inspect leaf undersides for thrips (tiny dark specks or silvery streaking) and mealybugs (white cottony deposits at leaf axils)
Verify the plant has at least 2–3 healthy leaves and shows signs of active growth
For listings showing only one photo, request images of the roots, underside of leaves, and any new growth before purchasing

fieldNotes.title: Cultivar of Ambition
fieldNotes.author: Aroid Aaron
fieldNotes.content: Philodendron 'Majestic' is a horticultural cultivar from tropical Central and South American ancestry — parentage undocumented with the typical informality of the collector cultivar trade. The name is doing some heavy lifting, but the plant has a certain ornamental presence through its large, dark, velvety-textured leaves that justifies the name's ambition somewhat. At accessible price tier. A decent decorative Philodendron for those who want something darker and more textured than hederaceum without moving into the serious collector tier.
